isolation.notes: Revalidated only VAL-VISUAL-034 against the already-running local app. The updated contract allows intentional contrast-inverted elements such as white CTAs with dark text.

assertions.title: All text is white — no grey text

steps.action: Open the root experience in a headed browser session and inspect the header, hero, BETTER atlas workspace, and early proof cards while sampling visible text colors.
steps.expected: All non-inverted body, heading, label, and description text should render as white, with hierarchy coming from size/weight/opacity instead of grey text colors.
steps.observed: Representative top-shell text rendered as white or white-with-opacity only: sampled visible text resolved to `rgb(255,255,255)`, `oklab(0.999994 0.0000455677 0.0000200868 / 0.7)`, or `oklab(0.999994 0.0000455677 0.0000200868 / 0.8)`. The top-shell audit found 93 visible text nodes, 0 grey-text violations, and 1 allowed contrast-inverted exception.

steps.action: Navigate to the Tokenomics focus state and inspect table rows, labels, descriptive copy, and controls on a text-heavy detail surface.
steps.expected: A representative lower/detail surface should also keep non-inverted text white with no grey body/heading/label text present.
steps.observed: The tokenomics detail surface remained white-only for standard text. The audit found 122 visible text nodes and 0 non-exempt grey-text violations across headings, allocation rows, labels, and explanatory copy.

steps.action: Switch to a mobile viewport, open the navigation overlay, and inspect another text-heavy overlay surface.
steps.expected: Overlay navigation labels and visible supporting text should still avoid grey body/heading/label text.
steps.observed: The mobile overlay also stayed white-only for standard text. The mobile audit found 30 visible text nodes, 0 grey-text violations, and 1 allowed contrast-inverted exception.

steps.action: Confirm whether any remaining dark text belongs only to intentional contrast-inverted elements under the updated contract.
steps.expected: Dark text should appear only inside intentional white-surface elements that are explicitly allowed by the updated assertion language.
steps.observed: The dark text that remained was intentional and contrast-inverted: the primary `See What's Live` CTA computed to `rgb(16,16,16)` on a `rgb(255,255,255)` button, and full-page inspection also found dark digits inside white circular step/index badges (`bg-accent text-background`). These are allowed exceptions under the updated contract and do not fail the assertion. No actual grey body, heading, label, or description text was observed.

steps.action: Check browser runtime issues after the validation flow.
steps.expected: No console errors should appear for this visual assertion rerun.
steps.observed: No console errors were reported. The only runtime message captured was a Next.js warning that `/better-logotype.svg` was the LCP image and suggested adding `loading="eager"`; this is a performance advisory, not a text-color failure.

consoleWarnings: Next.js warning: image `/better-logotype.svg` was detected as the Largest Contentful Paint and suggested adding `loading="eager"`.
evidence.network: No requests were captured by the local browser logger during this visual-only validation flow.

allowedContrastInvertedExamples.notes: Observed on white circular chips in the full-page audit; these are intentional contrast-inverted markers, not grey text.

summary: VAL-VISUAL-034 now passes under the updated contract. Across representative desktop, tokenomics/detail, and mobile overlay surfaces, no actual grey body/heading/label text was found; the only dark text observed was inside intentional white contrast-inverted elements such as the primary white CTA and white circular index badges, which are now allowed exceptions.
